title: Setaria schiedeana
taxonomy: Setaria schiedeana is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Setaria, which includes several species of grasses. The genus Setaria is further classified under the tribe Paniceae and subfamily Panicoideae. This classification places Setaria schiedeana within the larger group of grasses that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The detailed classification of Setaria schiedeana reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses.

intro: Setaria schiedeana, also known as Mexican bristle grass or sotolito, is a perennial grass native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, as well as potential uses in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and as an antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Setaria schiedeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Setaria schiedeana is typically used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
